content.title: Workflow Map
content.summary: This sample workflow represents an anonymized payment reconciliation and exception-handling process.

steps.description: Analyst receives or identifies an unmatched payment record.
steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

steps.notes: Generated by deterministic workflow mapper skeleton.

approval_references.title: Process Narrative
approval_references.snippet: The process involves identifying unmatched payment records, reviewing source-system information, classifying exceptions, escalating records requiring approval, and updating tracking records after resolution.

approval_references.title: Current Workflow Steps
approval_references.snippet: 6. Items above the approval threshold are routed to a supervisor.

approval_references.title: Policy and Controls
approval_references.snippet: - Payment exceptions above the approval threshold require supervisor approval.

approval_references.title: Policy and Controls
approval_references.snippet: - Write actions require explicit human approval.

approval_references.title: Sample Records
approval_references.snippet: REC-002,2026-01-04,8200.00,SystemB,Amount Mismatch,Pending Review,Supervisor,7,true,Amount exceeds approval threshold

classified_fields.rationale: Synthetic/internal record identifiers are allowed in the demo context.
Payment dates are financial transaction metadata and should be handled as internal financial data.
Payment amounts are financial data and require care, but synthetic demo values may be used.
Source-system labels are internal operational metadata.
Exception type is internal operational metadata.
Workflow status is internal operational metadata.
Assigned role is internal process metadata and does not identify an individual in the demo workflow.
Days open is internal process-aging metadata.
Supervisor review requirement is internal control metadata.
Free-text notes may contain sensitive data and should be reviewed or redacted.

approval_gates.name: Sensitive-data review before model context use
approval_gates.trigger: Input includes fields blocked from model context or requiring redaction.
approval_gates.agent_allowed_action: Use approved redacted context or request data handling review
approval_gates.blocked_action_without_approval: Send sensitive or unredacted data to model context

applies_to_steps.workflow_step: Analyst receives or identifies an unmatched payment record.
Analyst checks the source system for matching transaction details.
Analyst compares the record against an off-system tracking spreadsheet.
Items above the approval threshold are routed to a supervisor.
Supervisor reviews and approves or rejects the proposed resolution.

approval_gates.name: Human approval before operational write action
approval_gates.trigger: Agent recommendation would update workflow state, task assignments, customer-facing language, or system-of-record data.
approval_gates.human_reviewer: Process owner or authorized operations reviewer
approval_gates.blocked_action_without_approval: Execute write action or update system of record

applies_to_steps.workflow_step: Supervisor reviews and approves or rejects the proposed resolution.

approval_gates.name: Human approval before governed workflow advancement
approval_gates.trigger: Workflow item requires supervisor, process-owner, or policy-defined approval before advancing.
approval_gates.agent_allowed_action: Draft recommendation with rationale and required evidence
approval_gates.blocked_action_without_approval: Advance the workflow item, finalize a decision, or communicate a final outcome

escalation_rules.name: Escalate missing or conflicting source records
escalation_rules.condition: Required source information is missing, ambiguous, or conflicts with another system or workflow record.
escalation_rules.required_action: Manual review before the workflow item can be finalized.

applies_to_steps.workflow_step: Analyst checks the source system for matching transaction details.

escalation_rules.name: Escalate items requiring governed approval
escalation_rules.condition: Workflow item meets a policy, supervisor-review, sensitive-data, custom-scope, or elevated-risk condition.
escalation_rules.required_action: Authorized reviewer must approve, reject, or request clarification before the workflow item advances.

applies_to_steps.workflow_step: Items above the approval threshold are routed to a supervisor.
Supervisor reviews and approves or rejects the proposed resolution.

write_action_policy.rationale: Tool 'project_mgmt_server.create_issue' is write-capable and requires human approval.

backlog_items.title: Add sensitive-data redaction and model-context gate
backlog_items.description: Prevent fields requiring redaction or blocked from model context from being sent to the LLM without approved handling.

issue_creation_result.title: Add sensitive-data redaction and model-context gate
issue_creation_result.message: Dry run succeeded. No GitHub issue was created.

backlog_items.title: Add approval gate for governed operational decisions
backlog_items.description: Require authorized review before workflow items that meet policy-defined approval conditions are advanced, closed, or finalized.

backlog_items.title: Block operational write actions until approval is recorded
backlog_items.description: Ensure workflow state changes, task assignments, external communications, and system-of-record updates remain draft-only until an approved human decision is present.

backlog_items.title: Validate source records before workflow finalization
backlog_items.description: Prevent workflow items from being finalized when required source information is missing, ambiguous, or conflicting.

backlog_items.title: Create review queue for high-risk workflow items
backlog_items.description: Route high-risk workflow items to a review queue with required evidence, recommended reviewer, and approval status.

backlog_items.title: Implement escalation rules for blocked or high-risk workflow items
backlog_items.description: Escalate workflow items that meet high-risk conditions such as missing source information, required approval, sensitive data handling, or policy review.

backlog_items.title: Persist approval and write-action audit events
backlog_items.description: Move audit records for approvals, policy checks, and write actions from the MVP in-memory store to a durable append-only store.
